title: taraxacum praeradiantifrons
intro: Taraxacum praeradiantifrons, also known as the Asian dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain compounds like inulin, taraxasterol, and sesquiterpenes. The plant is used for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, offering health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and supporting liver function. Taraxacum praeradiantifrons is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or extract, and can also be consumed as a fresh juice or dried powder, although its safety and efficacy need further research and regulation.
